title: PD-EEG: Resting-State & Walking EEG in Parkinson's Disease
description: This dataset comprises 64-channel EEG recordings from 116 Parkinson's disease patients and 28 healthy controls acquired during resting state (sitting, eyes open) and treadmill walking conditions. Each participant underwent approximately 4 minutes of EEG recording under both behavioral conditions, with accompanying clinical assessments including MDS-UPDRS, MoCA, and medication dosage data. The dataset was collected at the Neurology Institute, Tel Aviv Sourasky Medical Center, and is organized according to BIDS specifications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 64-channel Geodesic EEG System 400 (EGI system) with electrode montage following the International 10–20 system. Recordings were conducted under two conditions: resting state (sitting, eyes open, ~4 minutes) and walking on a treadmill at comfortable speed (~4 minutes). Clinical assessments included demographic data, Levodopa Equivalent Daily Dose (LEDD), Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA), Movement Disorder Society Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS), and Color Trails Test (CTT).
